title: SQL Support on MySQL
text: SQL Support on MySQL Both the sql ProcScript command and SQL Editor are supported. Tthe MySQL connector fully supports the sql ProcScript command. You can perform almost any task with MySQL SQL Editor that you can accomplish using ProcScript. Allowed Commands For a complete list of SQL commands which cannot be executed within a user transaction, see your MySQL documentation. The network protocol you use can influence the type of commands that you are allowed to issue. Transaction Control The MQL connector does not change transaction state before entering and before leaving the sql ProcScript statement. Transaction processing should be avoided in SQL Editor and sql ProcScript, because the connector cannot manage a transaction which it did not initiate. The commands which involve transaction management are as follows: commit (ProcScript command) rollback (ProcScript command) commit transaction rollback transaction begin transaction Note: If you perform transaction management, once the transaction finishes, you must ensure that the logon path is returned to the same state that it was in before the transaction management was performed. If you do not do this, the MQL connector does not understand the correct state of the connection. Monitoring SQL in the Message Frame If requested in the application shell, the SQL generated by Uniface can be sent to the message frame. User-defined messages from stored procedures also appear here. Error messages from MySQL are always sent to the message frame. To receive all MySQL information messages, results, and user-defined messages from stored procedures in the message frame, set the /pri command line switch to 32 or higher. Determining Defined Keys Refer to the MySQL documentation. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
